merger_description:
Ursa 4 Kabushiki Kaisha, a special purpose vehicle indirectly owned and controlled by funds managed by Warburg Pincus LLC (**Warburg Pincus**), proposes to acquire J.S.B. Co., Ltd. (**JSB**).

Warburg Pincus is a private equity firm headquartered in New York, USA. Warburg Pincus has more than USD 100 billion in assets under management, and more than 215 companies in its active portfolio, diversified across stages, sectors, and geographies. In Australia, Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are primarily active in the software, financial and real estate sectors.

JSB is a provider of student housing and related services headquartered in Kyoto, Japan. JSB manages approximately 100,000 student housing units across 2,700 properties throughout Japan through its flagship brand, UniLife, and maintains longstanding relationships with more than 1,200 universities and institutions nationwide. JSB has recently incorporated an Australian subsidiary, J.S.B. AUS PTY LTD, but does not have any interests in entities that own or operate Australian residential real estate.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ursa 4 Kabushiki Kaisha, a special purpose vehicle indirectly owned
and controlled by funds managed by Warburg Pincus LLC (Warburg
Pincus), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of J.S.B. Co., Ltd.
(JSB), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of
the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Warburg Pincus is a private equity firm headquartered in New York,
USA. Warburg Pincus has more than USD 100 billion in assets under
management, and more than 215 companies in its active portfolio,
diversified across stages, sectors, and geographies. In Australia,
Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are primarily active in the
software, financial and real estate sectors.
The target, JSB, is a provider of student housing and related services
headquartered in Kyoto, Japan. JSB manages approximately 100,000
student housing units across 2,700 properties throughout Japan
through its flagship brand, UniLife, and maintains longstanding
relationships with more than 1,200 universities and institutions
nationwide. JSB has recently incorporated an Australian subsidiary,
J.S.B. AUS PTY LTD, but does not have any interests in entities that
own or operate Australian residential real estate.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no overlap between
Warburg Pincus and JSB in the supply of residential rental
accommodation in Australia, including built-to-rent property and
student rental accommodation.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
